prompt_preview:
You are a classical planning system. You are given a planning domain and a problem both in PDDL syntax.

STRICT REQUIREMENT: Your response must contain ONLY the executable plan — nothing else. No preamble, no explanation, no "We need to...", no natural-language commentary, no markdown unless it is a

message.content:
```pddl
(register_prescription primary_segment_1)
(reserve_inventory_for_prescription primary_segment_1 inventory_item_1)
(assign_formulary_task primary_segment_1 formulary_task_1)
(finalize_formulary_review primary_segment_1 formulary_task_1)
(stage_primary_segment_in_slot primary_segment_1 primary_slot_1 formulary_task_1)
(assign_staff_member primary_segment_1 staff_member_1)
(confirm_primary_packaging primary_segment_1 primary_slot_1 staff_member_1)
(unassign_staff_member primary_segment_1 staff_member_1)
(release_formulary_task primary_segment_1 formulary_task_1)
(register_prescription secondary_segment_1)
(reserve_inventory_for_prescription secondary_segment_1 inventory_item_2)
(assign_formulary_task secondary_segment_1 formulary_task_1)
(finalize_formulary_review secondary_segment_1 formulary_task_1)
(stage_secondary_segment_in_slot secondary_segment_1 secondary_slot_1 formulary_task_1)
(assign_staff_member secondary_segment_1 staff_member_1)
(confirm_secondary_packaging secondary_segment_1 secondary_slot_1 staff_member_1)
(assemble_dispense_package_from_slots primary_segment_1 secondary_segment_1 primary_slot_1 secondary_slot_1 dispense_package_1)
(release_formulary_task secondary_segment_1 formulary_task_1)
(assign_formulary_task primary_segment_1 formulary_task_1)
(seal_dispense_package dispense_package_1 primary_segment_1 formulary_task_1)
(release_formulary_task primary_segment_1 formulary_task_1)
(register_prescription workorder_1)
(reserve_inventory_for_prescription workorder_1 inventory_item_3)
(assign_formulary_task workorder_1 formulary_task_1)
(finalize_formulary_review workorder_1 formulary_task_1)
(apply_label_template_to_package workorder_1 label_template_1 dispense_package_1)
(initiate_labeling_process workorder_1 label_template_1 dispense_package_1 formulary_task_1)
(assign_equipment_check workorder_1 equipment_check_1)
(unassign_staff_member secondary_segment_1 staff_member_1)
(assign_staff_member workorder_1 staff_member_1)
(perform_equipment_check_start_labeling_variant_a workorder_1 equipment_check_1 staff_member_1 label_template_1 dispense_package_1)
(assign_regulatory_token workorder_1 auth_token_1)
(verify_regulatory_authorization_variant_a workorder_1 auth_token_1 label_template_1 dispense_package_1)
(mark_workorder_ready_for_finalization workorder_1)
(generate_and_attach_patient_instructions workorder_1 patient_instruction_1 formulary_task_1)
(release_workorder_for_pickup workorder_1 inventory_item_3 patient_instruction_1)
(release_formulary_task workorder_1 formulary_task_1)
(register_prescription workorder_2)
(reserve_inventory_for_prescription workorder_2 inventory_item_3)
(assign_formulary_task workorder_2 formulary_task_1)
(finalize_formulary_review workorder_2 formulary_task_1)
(apply_label_template_to_package workorder_2 label_template_2 dispense_package_1)
(initiate_labeling_process workorder_2 label_template_2 dispense_package_1 formulary_task_1)
(release_equipment_check workorder_1 equipment_check_1)
(assign_equipment_check workorder_2 equipment_check_1)
(unassign_staff_member workorder_1 staff_member_1)
(assign_staff_member workorder_2 staff_member_1)
(perform_equipment_check_start_labeling_variant_a workorder_2 equipment_check_1 staff_member_1 label_template_2 dispense_package_1)
(release_regulatory_token workorder_1 auth_token_1)
(assign_regulatory_token workorder_2 auth_token_1)
(verify_regulatory_authorization_variant_a workorder_2 auth_token_1 label_template_2 dispense_package_1)
(mark_workorder_ready_for_finalization workorder_2)
(generate_and_attach_patient_instructions workorder_2 patient_instruction_1 formulary_task_1)
(release_workorder_for_pickup workorder_2 inventory_item_3 patient_instruction_1)
(release_formulary_task workorder_2 formulary_task_1)
(finalize_primary_segment_for_release primary_segment_1 dispense_package_1)
(finalize_secondary_segment_for_release secondary_segment_1 dispense_package_1)
(assign_formulary_task primary_segment_1 formulary_task_1)
(generate_and_attach_patient_instructions primary_segment_1 patient_instruction_1 formulary_task_1)
(release_primary_segment_for_pickup primary_segment_1 inventory_item_1 patient_instruction_1)
(release_formulary_task primary_segment_1 formulary_task_1)
(assign_formulary_task secondary_segment_1 formulary_task_1)
(generate_and_attach_patient_instructions secondary_segment_1 patient_instruction_1 formulary_task_1)
(release_secondary_segment_for_pickup secondary_segment_1 inventory_item_2 patient_instruction_1)
```
message.reasoning:
The problem is a planning problem in PDDL for a pharmacy split dispense pickup staging scenario. I need to output only the executable plan as a sequence of ground PDDL actions, one per line.


## Understanding the Problem  
- The task focuses on a planning problem in PDDL for a pharmacy split dispense pickup staging scenario.  
- The goal is to generate an executable plan as a sequence of ground PDDL actions, with one action per line.

## Examining Work Orders  
- Workorder_1 and workorder_2 are associated with dispense_package_1 and include both primary_segment_1 and secondary_segment_1.  
- Each workorder uses a different label template: workorder_1 with label_template_1 and workorder_2 with label_template_2.  

## Reviewing Available Resources  
- Special handling options (1 and 2) and auxiliary labels (1 and 2) are available for use.  
- Patient_instruction_1, equipment_check_1, and auth_token_1 are available, while authorization_doc_1 is bound to workorder_1.  

## Assessing Authorization Status  
- Authorization_doc_2 is bound to workorder_2, but its availability is unclear, unlike authorization_doc_1, which is confirmed available.  
- This discrepancy may impact the release process for workorder_2.  

## Defining Release Requirements  
- To achieve "released_for_pickup" for segments and workorders, conditions like instructions_attached, inventory_reserved, and patient_instruction_linked must be met.  
- Actions such as generate_and_attach_patient_instructions and reserve_inventory_for_prescription are needed, requiring prerequisites like final_verification_completed and prescription_registered.  

## Clarifying Type Hierarchy  
- Prescription_order is a subtype of case_category, with dispense_segment_category and workorder_subcategory both under prescription_order.  
- Primary_dispense_segment, secondary_dispense_segment, and dispensing_workorder fit within this hierarchy, confirming their relation to prescription_order.

## Continuing Release Process  
- Patient_instruction and inventory_item availability is limited, requiring sequential processing for each segment and workorder to achieve "released_for_pickup."  
- Actions like generate_and_attach_patient_instructions consume and later free resources, suggesting a chaining approach to handle multiple items.  

## Managing Limited Resources  
- Only one formulary_task_1 is available, necessitating assignment to one segment, finalizing review, and releasing it before assigning to the next.  
- Similarly, one patient_instruction and equipment_check must be reused after completing and freeing each segment or workorder.  

## Planning Execution Steps  
- Start by registering and reserving inventory for primary_segment_1, then assign and finalize formulary_task_1 to set review_completed.  
- Next steps involve packaging actions like staging in slots, ensuring packaging_confirmed and prepared for release, given segments are already staged.

## Examining Workorder Authorization  
- Workorder_1 is bound to authorization_doc_1, which is available initially, allowing processing.  
- Workorder_2 is bound to authorization_doc_2, but doc_2 is unavailable, potentially blocking processing since binding requires document availability.  

## Exploring Workorder Processing Paths  
- Multiple paths exist for final_verification_completed, including authorization review, labeling, QC, and regulatory checks, suggesting flexibility.  
- Some paths, like authorization, may be optional, allowing workorder processing even if documents are unavailable.  

## Continuing Segment Preparation  
- After setting review_completed for primary_segment_1, staging in primary_slot_1 prepares it for packaging.  
- Assigning staff and confirming packaging sets primary_segment_packaging_confirmed and primary_segment_prepared, completing preparation without additional components.

## Continuing Segment Preparation
- To prepare both primary and secondary segments, formulary_task_1 must be assigned and later released to switch between segments.
- After preparing primary_segment_1, unassign staff and release the task to free it for secondary_segment_1, ensuring sequential processing.

## Sequencing Assembly Steps
- After preparing both segments, assemble the dispense package by linking primary_slot and secondary_slot, requiring both to be slot_ready.
- To seal the package, reassign formulary_task_1 to primary_segment_1, even after prior use, as sealing requires task assignment without needing review_completed.

## Handling Workorder Inventory
- Workorder_1 can reserve inventory_item_3, which is available after reserving items for primary and secondary segments.
- Limited resources like patient_instruction_1 suggest sequential processing for workorder_1 and workorder_2 to manage inventory constraints.

## Validating Workorder Authorization  
- Workorder_1 uses auth_token_1, which is assigned and not available, ensuring secure authorization.  
- Verifying regulatory authorization for workorder_1 sets workorder_authorization_verified, confirming compliance without completing labeling.  

## Finalizing Workorder Release  
- Marking workorder_1 ready for finalization sets final_verification_completed and final_release_marked, preparing it for release.  
- Generating and attaching patient instructions to workorder_1 using patient_instruction_1 and formulary_task_1 links them, making instructions_attached.  

## Releasing Workorder Components  
- Releasing workorder_1 for pickup with inventory_item_3 and patient_instruction_1 marks it as released, freeing up resources.  
- After release, inventory_item_3 and patient_instruction_1 become available again, but the task remains assigned to workorder_1.  

## Preparing for Workorder_2 Processing  
- To process workorder_2, release formulary_task_1 from workorder_1 to make it available for the next workorder.  
- Workorder_2 requires review completion and inventory reservation, with inventory_item_3 now available post-release.

## Reviewing Process Flow  
- The process confirms packaging wasn't used, relying on a confirm path without special handling, auxiliary labels, or authorization documents.  
- Regulatory token was used for labeling, skipping authorization_document_2, which was bound but not needed for workorder_2.

## Verifying Staff Assignment  
- Staff must be unassigned from secondary_segment_1 before assigning to workorder_1, ensuring no overlap in staff allocation.  
- Unassigning staff only requires the staff to be currently assigned, with no additional prerequisites needed.

## Checking Inventory Management  
- Workorder release requires inventory_reserved, with inventory_item_3 reserved for workorder_1 after prior segment reservations.

pddl2nl_prompt.note: autoplanbench_pddl2nl: run_save_descriptions.py --type full — шаблоны/JSON, без вызова LLM на построение промпта для сэмпла.

domain_setup.note: Файл domain_llm_usage_seedN.json не найден для section/subsection этого id (старый autoplanbench или домен не пересобирали). После run_domain_setup с обновлённым autoplanbench файл появится рядом с domain_description_seedN.json.

pipeline_usage.totals_scope_note: Per-run totals = planning + pddl2nl_prompt + nl2pddl (только tracked=true). domain_setup всегда вне totals (included_in_per_run_totals=false); цифры в блоке — для справки, агрегировать отдельно: domain_llm_usage_seed*.json + дедуп в collect_pipeline_token_usage.py.
